name: Greens
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the greens thoroughly through several changes of water, discarding any large, tough stems.
2. Heat the greens in just enough water to prevent sticking until they wilt.
3. To hasten wilting and prevent over-cooking, gently turn the greens over when steam begins to rise around the edges of the pan.
4. Cut through the greens several times with a sharp knife before packing them into jars, leaving 1-inch headspace.
5. Add ½ teaspoon salt per pint or 1 teaspoon per quart of greens.
6. Cover the greens with boiling water, leaving 1-inch headspace.
7. Remove air bubbles by tapping the jars and adjust the lids.
8. Process pints for 1 hour and 10 minutes or quarts for 1 hour and 30 minutes at 10 pounds pressure.
